Q502: Taxes should be directly proportional to the government needs/benefits received.  Property tax should pay for fire protection and local streets. Head tax should pay for police and government in general. Roads should be paid for with fuel taxes, tire taxes, milage taxes or wheel taxes. (not tolls on roads used by interstate travelers!) Income tax should only pay for something proportional to income and wealth, like education. If you didn't buy insurance for health or property, tough $#!+.  And if for humanitarian reasons we have to hand out government money after a disaster, the IRS should garnish their wages for the rest of their life to get it back. And there absolutely should be no death taxes, with only one reasonable but politically impossible exception; a death tax calculated to collect taxes that should have been paid, but weren't paid because the deceased voted for politicians who borrowed money instead of either reducing government expenditures or raising taxes. There should be no income taxes on businesses or corporations.
